claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children under the age of 16, or children under 20 who are in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have two children - Alex, who's 5 years old, and Blake, who's 10. They both live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have Alex (5 years old) and Blake (10 years old) living with you.

Now I need to ask: do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! That's helpful. 

Now, is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Do you have settled status, pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me review your situation:
- You have two children: Alex (5 years old) and Blake (10 years old)
- Both children live with you in the UK
- You are a British citizen living in the UK
- No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for either child

Based on the eligibility rules, you meet all the key criteria for Child Benefit for both children. You are responsible for children under 16 who live with you, you live in the UK, and you're a British citizen with the right to reside here. No one else is claiming for these children, and as only one person can claim Child Benefit per child, you are eligible to claim.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

You can now proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old, which is under 16. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant meets all eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 10 years old, which is under 16. Blake lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Blake as they live together. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Therefore, the claimant meets all eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK with two children under 16 (Alex, age 5, and Blake, age 10) who both live with them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children.

The claimant is responsible for Blake as they live together. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Therefore, the claimant meets all eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Blake.

No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children.
